message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_logsumexp autograd via apply_function with softmax-weighted backward [bd-t1q4]

tensor_logsumexp used to extract storage, compute y = log(sum_d
exp(x_d)) per (outer, inner) slice, and rebuild a
requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd pattern fixed
for cross / diag_embed / block_diag / matrix_norm / vander.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with the analytical backward:
    y = log(sum_d exp(x_d))
    dy/dx_d = exp(x_d) / sum_j exp(x_j) = exp(x_d - y)
    grad_x_d = grad_y * exp(x_d - y)
The exp(x - y) factor is in [0, 1] (since y >= max(x) under
max-subtract), so the backward is numerically stable without
needing a separate normalizer. Both x and y are saved; y is
already computed in the forward so saving it is free.

Adds logsumexp_propagates_gradient_via_softmax_weights regression
test: x = [1, 2, 3] reduced under sum loss → grad = softmax(x)
≈ [0.0900, 0.2447, 0.6652]. Includes a sanity check that the
gradient sums to 1.0 (softmax-distribution invariant).

Closes frankentorch-t1q4. ft-api lib tests: 1148 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-sre","title":"Native state dict serialization rejects F16/BF16 tensors","description":"testing-conformance-harnesses found a serialization contract gap in crates/ft-serialize/src/lib.rs. Native FrankenTorch state dict save/load only maps DType::F64 and DType::F32 to dtype tags, while ft-core DenseTensor and SafeTensors serialization already support DType::F16 and DType::BF16. This means half-precision tensors cannot round-trip through save_state_dict/load_state_dict even though the tensor stack supports them. Add stable native dtype tags for F16/BF16, encode/decode their 2-byte little-endian payloads, update the old unsupported-dtype test, and add round-trip coverage for F16 and BF16.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:41:25.813383663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:49:54.213972982Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:49:54.212224751Z","close_reason":"Completed: native state dict serialization now round-trips F16/BF16 tensors with stable dtype tags and byte-exact tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

2. Re-assess the training_step_memory, backward_heavy_allocation, and benchmark_family_tail regression budgets now that a stable baseline exists.
3. Decide whether the baseline should be refreshed again after any harness or kernel changes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T02:00:24.327228870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:43:49.037603064Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:43:49.037417603Z","close_reason":"Captured fresh perf-SLO measurement against 100x baseline. Remaining failures are matmul_core (expected for pure Rust), backward_pass, optimizer_step, checkpoint_roundtrip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-st4","title":"Refresh FEATURE_PARITY ledger after parity closure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:28:44.547921648Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:31:27.842733997Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:31:27.842368634Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-t1q4","title":"tensor_logsumexp is severed-autograd: dim-reducing softmax-weighted backward","description":"tensor_logsumexp at lib.rs:14728 computes y[outer, inner] = max + log(sum_d exp(x[outer, d, inner] - max)) but rebuilds the output as a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.logsumexp is differentiable.

Backward identity:
   y = log(sum_j exp(x_j))
   dy/dx_i = exp(x_i) / sum_j exp(x_j) = exp(x_i - y)
For the dim-reducing form:
   grad_x[outer, d, inner] = grad_y[outer, inner] * exp(x[outer, d, inner] - y[outer, inner])

The exp(x - y) factor is bounded in [0, 1] (since y >= max(x) in the max-subtract form), so the backward is numerically stable without needing a separate normalizer.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this analytical backward. Save x and y for backward — y is needed to construct the softmax weights without recomputing logsumexp. Surfaced during /reality-check pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_logsumexp in tensor_apply_function with the analytical backward grad_x_d = grad_y * exp(x_d - y). The exp(x - y) factor is in [0, 1] (since y >= max(x) under max-subtract), so the backward is numerically stable without a separate normalizer. Both x and y are saved for backward; the forward already computed y so it's free to save. Added logsumexp_propagates_gradient_via_softmax_weights regression test (x=[1,2,3] under sum loss → grad = softmax(x) ≈ [0.0900, 0.2447, 0.6652], with sanity sum-to-1 check). ft-api lib tests: 1148.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:30:23.669864847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:32:20.540082817Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:32:20.537491877Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-t33","title":"Document trait default method contracts with doc comments","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found 8 trait default methods that return trivial values. All are architecturally correct but several lack doc comments explaining WHY the default is what it is and WHEN implementors should override.

Prerequisite beads completed:
- frankentorch-jhi (CLOSED): Changed Optimizer::set_momentum to return Result
- frankentorch-tk5 (CLOSED): Added module_type to ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported, using std::any::type_name::<Self>() in the trait default

## Affected methods (8 total)

### Module trait (ft-nn/src/lib.rs)
Current state (verified 2026-04-03):

1. `named_parameters_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return this module's own named parameters (non-recursive)." + explanation about containers
   - NEEDS: "# Default behavior" and "# When to override" sections

2. `named_parameter_slots_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return dynamically-registered own parameters, including optional slots."
   - NEEDS: default/override guidance

3. `named_buffer_slots_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return own registered buffers, including persistence metadata."
   - NEEDS: default/override guidance

4. `named_children()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return direct child sub-modules with their local names." + naming conventions
   - NEEDS: explicit note that leaf modules should keep the default

5. `register_parameter()` -> Err(Unsupported{module_type, operation})
   - Has doc: "Register (or replace) a named parameter slot."
   - NEEDS: note about default returning Unsupported with module_type context, and when to override

6. `register_buffer()` -> Err(Unsupported{module_type, operation})
   - Has doc: "Register (or replace) a named buffer slot."
   - NEEDS: same as register_parameter

### Optimizer trait (ft-optim/src/lib.rs)
Current state (verified 2026-04-03):

7. `get_momentum()` -> None
   - Has doc: "Return momentum if the optimizer exposes one."
   - NEEDS: note that None means the optimizer has no momentum concept, and override when momentum exists (e.g., SGD)

8. `set_momentum()` -> Err(unsupported)
   - Has doc: "Set momentum for optimizers that expose it."
   - NEEDS: note about the Result return, when to override, and that non-momentum optimizers return Err

## Doc comment enhancement template
For EACH method, ENHANCE (do not replace) the existing doc comment by appending:

```rust
///
/// # Default behavior
/// Returns [specific value]. This is correct for [category of types] because [reason].
///
/// # When to override
/// Override this when [condition]. Example: [`Linear`] overrides this to [what it does].
```

Do NOT delete or modify existing doc comment text — only append the new sections.

## Plan
1. Read ft-nn/src/lib.rs and ft-optim/src/lib.rs to find exact current line numbers
2. For each of the 8 methods, append the default/override sections to the existing doc comment
3. Verify: `rch exec -- cargo doc -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --no-deps 2>&1` — zero warnings
4. Verify: `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn -p ft-optim -- -D warnings` — clean
5. Verify: `git diff` shows ONLY doc comment changes, zero logic modifications

## Testing requirements

### Automated verification
- `rch exec -- cargo doc -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --no-deps` — zero warnings
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn -p ft-optim` — all existing tests pass (doc changes can't break tests, but verify)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean

### Manual verification (implementing agent must confirm)
- [ ] All 8 methods have "# Default behavior" section
- [ ] All 8 methods have "# When to override" section
- [ ] No method signature or logic was changed
- [ ] Existing doc text was preserved (only appended to)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 methods have enhanced doc comments with default behavior + override guidance
- cargo doc clean
- cargo test clean
- cargo clippy clean
- git diff shows only comment additions

## Dependencies
- frankentorch-tk5 (CLOSED) — added module_type to Unsupported variant, docs must reflect this
- frankentorch-jhi (CLOSED) — changed set_momentum signature, docs must reflect Result return

## Reasoning
Future auditors (human or AI) encountering Vec::new() defaults may incorrectly flag them as stubs. Explicit "# Default behavior" sections prevent this and serve as self-documenting contracts.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:17.607354829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:05:57.884182107Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:05:57.884004772Z","close_reason":"Expanded default-trait docs for ft-nn Module traversal/registration hooks and ft-optim momentum hooks, then verified cargo doc/check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-t33","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-tk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:46.661582666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Critical for NLP models with sparse embeddings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:20.034772578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T06:05:37.104830719Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T06:05:37.104467241Z","close_reason":"Implemented SparseAdam optimizer with sparse-only moment updates, bias correction, validation. 4 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
